Title: AKR2A interacts with KCS1 to improve VLCFAs contents and chilling tolerance of Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Very-long-chain fatty acid (VLCFA) biosynthesis / fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ER fatty acid elongase complex; elongation of C18 fatty acids to C20-C24 VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KCS condensing enzyme family; transmembrane domain (residues 40–80)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Long-chain acyl-CoA (C18 fatty acid-derived acyl-CoA) and malonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3-ketoacyl-CoA elongated products leading to C20:0, C22:0 and C24:0 VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensation of malonyl-CoA with long-chain acyl-CoA to initiate each VLCFA elongation cycle, producing elongated 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Very-long-chain fatty acids (VLCFAs), especially C20:0, C22:0 and C24:0
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Helps maintain membrane stability and photosynthetic performance under chilling stress; contributes to stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for biosynthesis of the target VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/chain-length determining enzyme in VLCFA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaves; induced under chilling according to discussion and promoter analysis context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves; overexpression lines show elevated protein abundance; endogenous expression responds to chilling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in wild type and akr2a mutants; kcs1 knockout; fatty acid profiling by GC; western blot; qRT-PCR; yeast-2-hybrid; BiFC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases VLCFA accumulation and enhances chilling tolerance; overexpression partially rescues akr2a chilling-sensitive phenotype, whereas knockout reduces VLCFAs and tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to elevate VLCFAs and improve chilling tolerance; co-expression/stacking with AKR2A provides stronger effect than single-gene overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AKR2A affects KCS1 mainly at the protein level rather than transcript level; KCS1 overexpression does not fully rescue akr2a, implying additional KCS family members also depend on AKR2A
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KCS1 physically interacts with AKR2A via its transmembrane motif. 2. KCS1 protein abundance and VLCFA content are reduced in akr2a mutants. 3. KCS1 overexpression raises C20:0/C22:0 VLCFAs, improves membrane stability and photosynthetic efficiency, and enhances chilling tolerance. 4. Co-overexpression with AKR2A gives the strongest increase in VLCFAs and chilling tolerance.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ankyrin-repeat containing protein with N-terminal PEST motif
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: VLCFA biosynthesis via post-transcriptional control/stabilization and targeting of KCS1 and likely other KCS family proteins
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction-mediated stabilization/targeting of membrane enzyme; positive regulation at protein level
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes KCS1 protein accumulation/function; transcript of KCS1 is not significantly changed in akr2a mutants, but KCS1 protein is reduced
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive positive regulator required for maintaining VLCFA accumulation and chilling tolerance; co-overexpression with KCS1 gives maximal VLCFA increase and tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintain membrane integrity and photosynthetic capacity during chilling, thereby improving stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Chilling/cold stress; likely CBF-mediated cold signaling inferred from promoter cis-element analysis and expression kinetics
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression increases under chilling in seedlings, leaves, flowers and siliques
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by chilling; promoter-GUS and qRT-PCR show stronger expression under cold in multiple tissues
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter for GUS assay; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; RNAi construct for knockdown
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-GUS; qRT-PCR; akr2a TILLING mutants; RNAi knockdown; co-overexpression with KCS1; yeast-2-hybrid; BiFC; western blot; fatty acid profiling; chilling assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates VLCFA accumulation and chilling tolerance; loss or knockdown reduces KCS1 protein, lowers VLCFAs and causes chilling sensitivity, while overexpression increases tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown or mutation causes smaller plants, curled/chlorotic leaves and reduced growth
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering cold tolerance, especially combined with KCS1; caution that broad chaperone roles may affect growth/development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interaction occurs between AKR2A PEST motif (residues 1–40) and KCS1 transmembrane motif (residues 40–80); likely regulates additional KCS proteins beyond KCS1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AKR2A is induced by chilling. 2. AKR2A physically interacts with KCS1 through the PEST motif. 3. akr2a mutants show reduced KCS1 protein, lower VLCFAs and strong chilling sensitivity. 4. Co-overexpression of AKR2A and KCS1 produces the highest VLCFA levels and chilling tolerance.
